wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Yule Notion project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a modern full-stack note-taking application inspired by Notion, its core value proposition, target audience, and key differentiators. Document the overall architecture combining Vue 3 frontend with Node.js backend, highlighting the technology choices and design philosophy. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'page management', 'rich text editing', and 'offline synchronization'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the application's capabilities and user workflows. Document the project's positioning in the market and competitive advantages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Vue Router configuration and navigation system. Document the route definitions, navigation guards, and authentication protection mechanisms. Explain the route hierarchy, lazy loading strategies, and dynamic route parameters. Detail the implementation of protected routes, redirect logic, and error handling for navigation failures. Include examples of programmatic navigation, route transitions, and breadcrumb navigation patterns. Document the integration with authentication state and how routes are conditionally displayed based on user permissions. Address performance optimizations like route-level code splitting and preloading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Express routing system architecture. Detail the route organization pattern with modular route creation using factory functions like createAuthRouter(). Explain the base URL structure (/api/v1/) and versioning strategy. Document route registration patterns, parameter handling, and middleware attachment to specific routes. Include examples of route definition patterns, error propagation through routes, and integration with controllers. Address route security considerations, validation middleware integration, and performance optimization techniques for route matching.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Definition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive schema definition documentation for the PostgreSQL database tables. Detail each table structure including users, pages, tags, page_tags, uploaded_files, and sync_log tables. Document primary keys, foreign keys, constraints, and data types for every column. Explain the UUID-based primary key strategy and why it was chosen over auto-incrementing integers. Cover the JSONB data modeling approach for page content storage, including the TipTap editor content format and validation rules. Document the soft deletion mechanism with is_deleted flag and deleted_at timestamp. Include field-level comments and descriptions explaining the business purpose of each column. Provide examples of typical data structures stored in each table.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Authentication Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the backend authentication service implementation. Detail the JWT token generation and validation process, including secret key management and token expiration handling. Document the user registration workflow with email validation, password hashing using BcryptJS, and database operations for user creation. Explain the login process including credential verification, token issuance, and session management. Cover the authentication middleware implementation for protecting routes and extracting user information from tokens. Include error handling strategies, input validation with Zod schemas, and security measures against common vulnerabilities. Provide code examples showing service method implementations and controller integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Editor Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core TipTapEditor component implementation. Detail the editor initialization process, extension configuration, and state management patterns. Explain the editor lifecycle, content serialization/deserialization, and real-time update handling. Document the editorProps configuration including custom attributes, keyboard shortcuts, and paste handling. Cover the content synchronization mechanism between the editor and page data, including conflict resolution and automatic saving. Address performance considerations for large documents, memory management, and cleanup procedures. Include practical examples of extending the editor with custom extensions and handling edge cases in content rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the frontend page state management system. Detail the Pinia store implementation including state properties (pages, currentPageId, loading), getters (currentPage, rootPages, getChildren), and actions (createPage, updatePage, deletePage, setCurrentPage). Explain the local storage persistence mechanism, ID generation strategy, and default content creation. Document the reactive data flow, computed properties for derived state, and state synchronization patterns. Include examples of store usage in components, state mutations, and integration with the TipTap editor. Address performance considerations, memory management, and state hydration during application startup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints in Yule Notion. Document the three core authentication endpoints: POST /api/v1/auth/register for user registration with email/password/name validation, POST /api/v1/auth/login for user authentication returning JWT tokens, and GET /api/v1/auth/me for retrieving current user information. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error responses. Include detailed parameter descriptions with validation rules, example requests/responses, and security considerations. Document JWT token structure, expiration handling, and refresh strategies. Address common authentication scenarios, rate limiting policies, and integration patterns for client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Storage Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the local storage layer implementation using IndexedDB and Dexie.js. Explain the database schema design including tables for pages, tags, syncQueue, and meta data. Document the Dexie database class configuration, table definitions, and indexing strategies. Detail the LocalPage interface with both shared fields and local-only extensions like _syncStatus and _dirty flags. Include examples of CRUD operations, transaction handling, and data persistence patterns. Address database versioning, migration strategies, and data integrity mechanisms. Provide code examples showing how to query, update, and manage local data efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Upload Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frontend image upload component. Explain the component's architecture, props interface, and event handling mechanisms. Document the file selection workflow, drag-and-drop functionality, and paste-to-upload features. Detail the integration with TipTap editor for seamless media insertion. Include the component's styling approach, dark theme adaptation, and accessibility considerations. Provide usage examples showing how to integrate the component into different editor contexts. Address file validation, progress indication, and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend testing documentation for Yule Notion's Vue 3 application. Document the testing setup using Vue Test Utils, component testing strategies, and store testing approaches. Explain how to test TipTap editor components, sidebar navigation, authentication flows, and page management components. Include examples of mocking Vue dependencies, testing reactive state changes, and simulating user interactions. Detail the testing utilities, helper functions, and assertion patterns used in the frontend test suite. Address testing component lifecycle, event handling, and prop validation. Provide guidelines for testing asynchronous operations, form validation, and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PostgreSQL full-text search implementation. Detail the tsvector-based indexing strategy, multilingual text processing with pg_trgm, and relevance scoring algorithms. Document the database schema modifications including the search_vector column, GIN indexes, and trigger functions. Explain the search operators @@, @@, and websearch_to_tsquery for different query types. Cover the search ranking mechanism using ts_rank and custom weight configurations. Include examples of search queries, performance optimization techniques, and maintenance procedures for keeping search indexes current. Address search performance tuning, memory usage optimization, and scalability considerations for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Upload Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ImageUpload component implementation. Explain the file upload workflow, including drag-and-drop support, file validation, and preview generation. Document the integration with the backend upload API, including authentication, progress tracking, and error handling. Cover image processing options, compression settings, and supported formats. Detail the component's interaction with the TipTap editor for image insertion and positioning. Include examples of customizing upload behavior, implementing custom upload handlers, and handling upload failures. Address security considerations, file size limitations, and performance optimization for large images.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CI/CD pipeline documentation for Yule Notion. Document the GitHub Actions workflow configuration including build processes, automated testing, and deployment automation. Explain the frontend build pipeline with Vite, TypeScript compilation, and asset optimization. Detail the backend build process with TypeScript transpilation and dependency management. Include environment variable management, secret handling, and artifact deployment strategies. Document the testing integration within the pipeline, including unit tests, integration tests, and quality gates. Provide examples of pipeline customization, branch protection rules, and deployment triggers. Address troubleshooting common pipeline failures and optimization techniques for faster builds.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Tokens & Color System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the design tokens and color system. Explain the semantic color palette, typography scale, spacing units, and border radius tokens used throughout the application. Document the relationship between design tokens and CSS custom properties, including how tokens cascade through the component hierarchy. Detail the color contrast ratios, accessibility compliance, and WCAG guidelines adherence. Include examples of using design tokens in components, creating new tokens, and maintaining design consistency. Address the integration with UnoCSS utilities and how design tokens map to utility classes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Listing and Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for page listing endpoints including GET /api/v1/pages with tree/flattened response modes. Document the tree parameter for nested structure responses, parentId filtering for specific folder hierarchies, and includeDeleted option for retrieving soft-deleted pages. Explain the differences between flat list responses (containing full metadata) and tree responses (optimized for sidebar navigation). Detail pagination parameters, response schemas for both modes, and performance considerations for large page collections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Yule Notion development setup. Include detailed environment requirements (Node.js v20+, PostgreSQL 14+, Redis optional), step-by-step installation instructions for both frontend and backend, and development environment configuration. Document the complete setup process from cloning the repository to running the application locally. Provide clear instructions for database setup, environment variable configuration, and dependency installation. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Address both development and production deployment scenarios with practical examples from the actual codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Pinia state management system. Detail the store architecture, state structure, and action implementations for authentication, page management, and theme handling. Explain the reactive state patterns, computed properties, and state persistence strategies. Document the store composition patterns, async action handling, and state synchronization with the backend API. Include examples of store usage in components, state mutations, and cross-store communication. Address state hydration from localStorage, error handling in store actions, and performance optimization techniques like selective reactivity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Controllers Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the controller layer implementation. Explain the controller pattern with clear separation of concerns between request handling, business logic delegation, and response formatting. Document the controller lifecycle including request validation, service method invocation, and standardized response/error handling. Detail error handling patterns, HTTP status code usage, and response payload structures. Include examples of controller method signatures, parameter extraction, and integration with service layer. Address controller testing strategies and common implementation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed entity relationship documentation showing how the database tables interact with each other. Document the relationships between users and pages (one-to-many), users and tags (one-to-many), pages and tags through the junction table (many-to-many), and users and uploaded_files (one-to-many). Explain the adjacency list model used for hierarchical page organization through parent_id references. Detail the referential integrity constraints and cascading behaviors. Show how the soft deletion mechanism affects relationships and query patterns. Include entity-relationship diagrams and explain the rationale behind the chosen relationships. Document the optimistic locking strategy using version columns and its impact on relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Authentication Store
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the frontend authentication state management using Pinia stores. Explain the authentication state structure including user data, token storage, and login status tracking. Document the authentication service methods for API integration, including login/logout handlers and token persistence strategies. Detail the Vue Router navigation guards for protecting authenticated routes and redirecting unauthenticated users. Cover token refresh mechanisms, automatic logout on token expiration, and error handling for authentication failures. Include practical examples of authentication hooks, composables for reactive authentication state, and integration patterns with Vue components. Address local storage security considerations and token cleanup strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Editor Extensions & Extensions System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the TipTap extension system and available editor extensions. Explain the extension configuration for StarterKit, Placeholder, Link, Image, Underline, TextAlign, TextStyle, Color, Highlight, TaskList, TaskItem, and Table extensions. Document the extension lifecycle, configuration options, and customization patterns. Detail how extensions interact with each other and the editor state. Cover extension-specific features like table resizing, task list nesting, and placeholder text customization. Include examples of adding custom extensions, configuring existing extensions, and troubleshooting extension conflicts. Address performance implications of different extension combinations and best practices for extension selection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sidebar Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the sidebar navigation system. Explain the hierarchical page display using the PageList component, tree rendering with parent-child relationships, and recursive component structure. Document the NewNoteButton functionality for creating new pages, drag-and-drop reordering capabilities, and page selection mechanisms. Detail the sidebar layout, responsive behavior, and integration with the main dashboard view. Include examples of page tree traversal, indentation logic, and visual indicators for page hierarchy. Address user interaction patterns, keyboard navigation support, and accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for page management endpoints in Yule Notion. Document all CRUD operations including GET /api/v1/pages for listing pages with tree/flattened modes, POST /api/v1/pages for creating new pages, GET /api/v1/pages/:id for retrieving individual pages, PUT /api/v1/pages/:id for updating pages with optimistic locking via If-Match headers, DELETE /api/v1/pages/:id for soft deletion, and PUT /api/v1/pages/:id/move for reorganizing page hierarchy. Include comprehensive request/response schemas, query parameters for filtering and pagination, validation rules for page properties (title, content, icon, parentId, order), and conflict resolution strategies. Document the TipTap JSON content format, version control mechanism, and recursive deletion behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sync Engine Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the core synchronization engine. Explain the SyncEngine class architecture, including network detection, sync scheduling, and state management. Document the main sync workflow: detecting changes, fetching server updates, applying local changes, and handling conflicts. Detail the Last-Write-Wins (LWW) strategy implementation and conflict resolution mechanisms. Include the sync scheduler functionality for automatic triggering on network events. Provide code examples showing sync initiation, progress tracking, and error handling. Address concurrent sync prevention, retry mechanisms, and graceful degradation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Upload Controller
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the backend upload controller implementation. Detail the controller's role in handling file upload requests, request validation, and response formatting. Document the supported file types, size limits, and security validation processes. Explain the integration with file storage systems and database metadata management. Include error handling strategies, authentication requirements, and rate limiting considerations. Provide examples of controller method signatures, parameter validation, and response structures. Address CORS configuration and cross-origin request handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend testing documentation for Yule Notion's Node.js/Express application. Document the testing setup with Jest, controller testing strategies, and service layer testing approaches. Explain how to test authentication endpoints, database operations, middleware functions, and error handling scenarios. Include examples of mocking database connections, testing API routes, and validating request/response schemas. Detail the testing utilities, database cleanup strategies, and test environment configuration. Address testing authentication flows, authorization checks, and data validation. Provide guidelines for testing asynchronous database operations, transaction handling, and error propagation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Search Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the frontend search service implementation. Explain the search API integration, request debouncing strategies, and real-time search suggestions. Document the search service methods, parameter handling, and response processing. Detail the integration with the pages store for displaying search results and updating the UI. Cover the search input validation, error handling, and loading states. Include examples of search service usage, integration patterns with Vue components, and performance optimizations like request cancellation and result caching. Address the search UI components that consume this service and how they handle search result display and navigation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Emoji Picker Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the EmojiPicker component implementation. Explain the emoji selection interface, including category navigation, search functionality, and keyboard shortcuts. Document the emoji data management, including emoji categorization, Unicode handling, and fallback mechanisms. Cover the integration with the editor for emoji insertion and positioning. Detail the component's state management, including recent emojis, favorites, and search history. Include examples of customizing emoji themes, adding custom emoji sets, and implementing emoji filtering. Address accessibility considerations, internationalization support, and performance optimization for large emoji datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization & Docker
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed containerization documentation for Yule Notion. Document the Docker containerization strategy for both frontend and backend services. Explain multi-stage Docker builds, base image selection, and layer optimization techniques. Detail container orchestration patterns, networking configuration, and volume management. Include Docker Compose setup for local development and staging environments. Document container security practices, resource limits, and health check implementations. Provide examples of container registry integration, image scanning, and vulnerability management. Address container scaling strategies, load balancing, and service discovery patterns. Include troubleshooting container deployment issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme Store & State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the theme store implementation using Pinia. Explain the theme mode management (light, dark, system), localStorage persistence, and reactive theme switching. Document the store's API including setMode(), toggle(), and state properties. Detail the system preference detection, media query listeners, and automatic theme resolution. Include examples of integrating the theme store with Vue components, handling theme changes in real-time, and implementing theme-aware UI patterns. Address performance considerations, memory management, and browser compatibility for theme detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Creation and Initial Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for page creation via POST /api/v1/pages endpoint. Document all creation parameters including title defaults, TipTap JSON content structure with empty document format, parentId for hierarchical placement, order for sorting, and icon selection. Explain default values, validation rules, and the automatic assignment of initial properties like version number and timestamps. Include examples of creating root pages versus child pages, and demonstrate proper TipTap JSON structure for different content types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for Yule Notion. Detail the complete frontend stack including Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router, Vite, UnoCSS, and Lucide Icons, explaining the rationale behind each choice and their roles in the application architecture. Document the backend stack featuring Express.js, PostgreSQL with Knex.js ORM, JWT authentication, BcryptJS, Pino logging, Zod validation, and security middleware. Include version requirements, compatibility considerations, and upgrade paths. Explain the integration patterns between frontend and backend technologies, dependency relationships, and how the stack supports the application's core features like rich text editing and offline synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Vue 3 component architecture and design patterns. Document the component hierarchy, composition patterns, and reusable component design. Explain the component lifecycle, prop definitions, event handling, and slot usage patterns. Detail the implementation of complex components like the TipTapEditor, Sidebar, and PageEditor with their child components. Include examples of component composition, dependency injection through provide/inject, and component communication patterns. Address component performance optimization, memory management, and accessibility considerations. Document the component styling approach and CSS module usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Services Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the service layer architecture and business logic implementation. Explain the service pattern with dependency injection, transaction management, and business rule enforcement. Document service method design patterns, error handling strategies, and data transformation between layers. Detail the service lifecycle including input validation, business logic execution, and output formatting. Include examples of service method implementation, error propagation, and integration with database layer. Address service testing approaches, mocking strategies, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexes & Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database indexing strategy and performance optimization. Detail the purpose and structure of each index including composite indexes on user_id with parent_id and order for efficient page hierarchy queries. Explain the GIN indexes used for JSONB content searching and full-text search vector optimization. Document the trigger functions that automatically maintain search vectors and updated_at timestamps. Cover the PostgreSQL full-text search implementation using tsvector and the custom function that extracts text from TipTap JSON content. Explain the performance implications of each index choice and query optimization patterns. Include recommendations for monitoring query performance and identifying missing indexes.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for JWT token lifecycle management in the application. Explain token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms including payload structure, expiration handling, and security considerations. Document token storage strategies on both frontend (localStorage/sessionStorage) and backend (HTTP-only cookies) with security implications. Detail the token refresh process for maintaining user sessions without re-authentication. Cover token validation middleware implementation, header parsing, and error handling for invalid/expired tokens. Include practical examples of token extraction, verification, and renewal workflows. Address security best practices such as token signing algorithms, secret key management, and protection against token theft attacks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formatting Tools & UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the formatting tools and UI components that provide rich text editing capabilities. Document the ColorPicker component for text and highlight color selection, including color validation and persistence. Explain the AlignDropdown component for text alignment options (left, center, right, justify). Detail the HeadingDropdown component for hierarchical heading selection and styling. Cover the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and event handling mechanisms. Include examples of integrating these components with the main editor, customizing their appearance, and extending their functionality. Address accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation, and responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Page Controller
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the backend page controller and related services. Document the CRUD operations implementation, request validation, and response formatting. Explain the database interactions, query optimization, and transaction handling. Detail the authentication middleware integration, authorization checks, and data sanitization. Include examples of API endpoint implementations, error handling strategies, and data transformation patterns. Address the service layer architecture, dependency injection, and business logic encapsulation. Document the route definitions, parameter validation, and response status codes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for search functionality endpoints. Document the GET /api/v1/search endpoint for full-text search across titles and content, including query parameters (q, page, pageSize, tagId), search algorithms, ranking mechanisms, and result formatting with highlighted matches. Explain the PostgreSQL full-text search implementation, weighting strategies favoring title matches over content matches, snippet generation with keyword highlighting, and pagination support. Include examples of search queries, result schemas with highlighted text, and integration patterns for front-end search interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conflict Resolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the conflict resolution system. Explain the Last-Write-Wins (LWW) strategy implementation and timestamp-based decision making. Document how conflicts are detected, categorized, and resolved automatically. Detail the user notification system for manual conflicts and the fallback mechanisms. Include examples of different conflict scenarios: concurrent edits, network splits, and data corruption recovery. Address the _syncStatus field management and how conflicts affect local data state. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common conflict resolution issues and manual intervention procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upload Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the upload service layer implementation. Explain the service's responsibility for file processing, storage management, and metadata operations. Document the file validation algorithms, security scanning integration, and virus detection mechanisms. Detail the storage abstraction layer, file naming conventions, and organizational strategies. Include the service's integration with database operations, file system management, and cleanup procedures. Address performance optimization techniques, concurrent upload handling, and memory management. Provide examples of service method implementations and error recovery strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Test Reporting & Coverage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed test reporting and coverage documentation for Yule Notion's quality assurance processes. Document the test execution workflow, coverage metrics collection, and reporting mechanisms. Explain how to interpret test results, analyze coverage reports, and track test performance over time. Include examples of test report formats, coverage thresholds, and quality gates. Detail the continuous integration testing pipeline, automated test execution, and failure reporting. Address test maintenance strategies, flaky test detection, and test suite optimization. Provide guidelines for setting up test monitoring, tracking regressions, and maintaining test quality as the codebase evolves.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Performance & Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for search performance optimization strategies and best practices. Document query optimization techniques, index maintenance procedures, and search algorithm tuning. Explain caching strategies for frequently searched terms, result pagination, and lazy loading of search results. Cover performance monitoring approaches, query execution plans analysis, and bottleneck identification. Detail the trade-offs between search accuracy and performance, including stemming algorithms and fuzzy matching configurations. Include benchmarking methodologies, load testing scenarios, and capacity planning guidelines. Address search result ranking optimization, relevance tuning, and user experience improvements through faster search responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Link Dialog Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the LinkDialog component implementation. Explain the link creation and editing workflow, including URL validation, target configuration, and link text customization. Document the integration with the TipTap Link extension, including link state detection and manipulation. Cover the dialog's form validation, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Detail the component's interaction with the editor for link insertion and modification. Include examples of customizing link validation rules, implementing custom link handlers, and supporting different link types. Address security considerations, URL sanitization, and accessibility compliance for link dialogs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive production deployment documentation for Yule Notion. Document the production environment setup, infrastructure requirements, and deployment topology. Explain environment configuration management, secrets handling, and security hardening practices. Detail database deployment, migration strategies, and backup procedures. Include load balancer configuration, SSL/TLS setup, and reverse proxy implementation. Document monitoring and logging infrastructure, health check endpoints, and alerting mechanisms. Provide deployment checklists, rollback procedures, and disaster recovery plans. Address scaling considerations, auto-scaling policies, and performance optimization. Include examples of environment-specific configurations, capacity planning, and cost optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSS Architecture & Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the CSS architecture and file organization. Explain the global styles structure, component-scoped styling patterns, and CSS custom property usage. Document the CSS-in-JS integration with UnoCSS, utility-first class naming conventions, and style encapsulation strategies. Detail the responsive design breakpoints, flexbox/grid patterns, and layout systems. Include guidelines for writing maintainable CSS, avoiding specificity conflicts, and implementing consistent styling across components. Address CSS optimization techniques, bundle size considerations, and development workflow integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Single Page Retrieval and Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for single page retrieval using GET /api/v1/pages/:id endpoint. Document the complete response schema including full content with TipTap JSON, metadata fields, and associated data. Explain access control mechanisms preventing users from accessing other users' pages, error handling for non-existent resources, and the distinction between deleted and inaccessible pages. Include examples of retrieving pages with different content types and demonstrate proper error responses for unauthorized access attempts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Vue 3 frontend application. Describe the high-level design following the Composition API pattern, component hierarchy, and application structure. Document the routing configuration with Vue Router, navigation guards, and route protection mechanisms. Explain the state management architecture using Pinia stores for authentication, page management, and theme handling. Detail the build configuration with Vite, TypeScript integration, and development workflow. Include component organization patterns, dependency injection through composables, and the separation of concerns between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Address performance optimization strategies and development best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Vite build system and development configuration. Detail the Vite configuration options, plugin integrations, and build optimization strategies. Explain the TypeScript compilation settings, path aliases, and type checking configurations. Document the development server setup, hot module replacement, and proxy configurations for API requests. Include examples of build customization, asset optimization, and production deployment configurations. Address environment variable handling, conditional compilation, and build performance optimization techniques. Document the integration with UnoCSS, Vue SFC processing, and development workflow automation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the middleware architecture and execution pipeline. Detail the middleware stack order and execution flow from outer to inner layers. Document each middleware component including helmet for security headers, cors for cross-origin handling, rateLimit for request throttling, and pinoHttp for request logging. Explain custom middleware implementation patterns, error handling middleware, and validation middleware. Include examples of middleware chaining, async middleware handling, and performance impact considerations. Address middleware testing strategies and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migrations & Versioning
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the database migration system using Knex.js. Explain the migration file structure, naming conventions, and execution order. Document the initial migration that creates the complete database schema including all tables, indexes, triggers, and constraints. Cover the migration workflow from development to production, including how to create new migrations for schema changes. Explain rollback procedures and best practices for safe database updates. Detail the configuration in knexfile.ts and how migrations are integrated into the deployment pipeline. Include examples of common migration patterns and troubleshooting migration issues. Document the relationship between database versioning and application versioning.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Security & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for password security implementation and validation. Detail the BcryptJS integration for secure password hashing including salt generation and cost factor configuration. Document input validation strategies using Zod schemas for email format, password strength requirements, and name validation. Explain the password verification process during login including hash comparison and timing attack prevention. Cover security measures such as rate limiting for authentication attempts, input sanitization, and protection against brute force attacks. Include practical examples of password validation rules, hashing implementations, and security middleware usage. Address common security vulnerabilities and their mitigation strategies in authentication flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Insertion & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for media insertion and management features. Detail the ImageUpload component for image handling, including file validation, drag-and-drop support, and integration with the backend file upload system. Explain the EmojiPicker component for emoji insertion, including emoji categories, search functionality, and keyboard shortcuts. Document the LinkDialog component for URL insertion and management, including link validation and target configuration. Cover the media insertion workflow, from user interaction to content rendering. Include examples of customizing media handling, implementing custom upload providers, and managing media metadata. Address security considerations, file size limits, and performance optimization for media-heavy content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Content Storage
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for page content storage and data persistence. Explain the TipTap JSON content format, database schema design for storing page content, and data serialization strategies. Document the local storage implementation for offline functionality, IndexedDB integration, and synchronization mechanisms. Detail the content validation, sanitization, and security considerations. Include examples of content migration, backup strategies, and data integrity checks. Address the hybrid storage approach combining local and remote storage, conflict resolution for concurrent edits, and performance optimization for large content volumes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tag Management Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for tag management endpoints covering GET /api/v1/tags for retrieving all user tags, POST /api/v1/tags for creating new tags with unique naming constraints, DELETE /api/v1/tags/:id for removing tags and cleaning associations, PUT /api/v1/pages/:id/tags for bulk tag assignment to pages, and GET /api/v1/tags/:id/pages for retrieving tagged pages. Document tag creation validation rules, uniqueness enforcement per user, automatic cleanup of tag associations on deletion, and the atomic nature of tag assignment operations. Include examples of tag schemas, error handling for conflicts, and pagination for tag page listings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Auto-Save System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the automatic saving mechanism that provides seamless offline editing experience. Explain the debounced save implementation with 1-second delay, optimistic updates, and immediate local persistence. Detail the integration with IndexedDB for instant writes and the sync queue population process. Document the _dirty flag management, save status indicators, and user feedback mechanisms. Include examples of the auto-save composable usage, configuration options, and performance optimizations. Address edge cases like rapid typing, memory management, and save failure recovery. Provide guidance on customizing auto-save behavior and handling large document updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the file storage management system. Explain the directory structure, file organization strategies, and naming conventions used for uploaded files. Document the storage backend configuration, disk space management, and cleanup procedures. Detail the file access patterns, URL generation, and content delivery optimization. Include security measures for file access control, permission management, and malicious file prevention. Address backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and storage scaling considerations. Provide examples of storage configuration, file retrieval patterns, and performance monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: UnoCSS Configuration & Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for UnoCSS configuration and utility class system. Explain the configuration options, preset usage, and custom utility generation. Document the utility class naming conventions, responsive variants, and state modifiers. Detail the integration with Vue components, dynamic class binding, and conditional styling patterns. Include examples of creating custom utilities, extending existing presets, and optimizing bundle size. Address performance implications of utility-first CSS, build optimization strategies, and debugging utility conflicts. Provide guidelines for maintaining utility class consistency and avoiding bloat.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Updates and Optimistic Locking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the page update mechanism via PUT /api/v1/pages/:id endpoint with comprehensive coverage of optimistic locking using If-Match headers. Explain the version-based conflict detection system, proper header usage patterns, and conflict resolution strategies. Detail partial update capabilities for title, content, and icon fields, TipTap JSON replacement requirements, and automatic version incrementing. Include practical examples of concurrent editing scenarios, proper error handling for version mismatches, and best practices for implementing optimistic locking in client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Node.js/Express backend application. Describe the layered architecture with clear separation between controllers, services, and data access layers. Document the middleware stack including authentication, error handling, and validation middleware. Explain the dependency injection patterns, service layer organization, and database abstraction through Knex.js ORM. Detail the Express application configuration, route organization, and API endpoint structure. Include security implementations with JWT authentication, password hashing, and input validation. Address logging strategies with Pino, error handling patterns, and performance monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database integration architecture using Knex.js ORM. Detail the database connection management, configuration patterns, and environment-specific settings. Document the migration system with timestamp-based migration files and rollback strategies. Explain query building patterns, transaction handling, and connection pooling. Include examples of database operations, model representation, and data access patterns. Address database security considerations, connection management, and performance optimization techniques. Document the migration workflow and database schema evolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Slash Command System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the slash command system implementation. Explain the command menu trigger mechanism, including keyboard detection, position calculation, and visibility management. Document the available commands including paragraphs, headings, lists, blocks, and media insertion. Cover the command filtering and search functionality, including query matching and result ranking. Detail the command execution workflow, from menu selection to content insertion. Explain the command menu positioning system, including viewport awareness and collision detection. Include examples of adding custom commands, implementing command categories, and extending the command system. Address performance considerations for command lookup and menu rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for file upload functionality. Document the POST /api/v1/upload/image endpoint for image uploads using multipart/form-data, including supported file formats (jpg/png/gif/webp), size limitations (≤ 5MB), storage strategies, and URL generation. Explain the file naming convention using UUID prefixes, static file serving through Nginx, and direct public access URLs. Include client-side upload examples, error handling for unsupported formats and size limits, and integration patterns for embedding uploaded images in TipTap content. Address security considerations for file access and storage management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for network connectivity management and handling. Explain the network detection mechanisms using navigator.onLine API and event listeners. Document the automatic sync triggering on network restoration and manual sync controls. Detail the offline state detection, connection status monitoring, and network error handling strategies. Include examples of network-aware UI components, loading states, and user notifications. Address background sync processes, retry logic for failed operations, and graceful handling of intermittent connectivity. Provide troubleshooting guidance for network-related issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Soft Deletion and Hierarchical Effects
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for page deletion mechanisms including the DELETE /api/v1/pages/:id endpoint behavior. Explain the soft delete implementation using is_deleted flag and timestamp recording, recursive deletion effects on child pages, and cascade behavior throughout the page hierarchy. Document the 204 No Content response format, access control for deletion operations, and the impact on search indexing and navigation. Include examples of deleting root pages versus child pages and demonstrate the hierarchical cascade effect on page organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the PostgreSQL database schema. Detail the entity relationships between users, pages, tags, and uploaded files, including primary keys, foreign keys, and constraints. Document the field definitions, data types, and validation rules for each table. Explain the JSONB data modeling approach used for page content storage and the indexing strategy for full-text search capabilities. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document the migration system with Knex.js, version control for database schema changes, and rollback procedures. Address data integrity constraints, referential integrity, and performance optimization through proper indexing and query patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the configuration management system. Explain environment variable handling, configuration loading patterns, and environment-specific settings. Document the configuration structure including database credentials, security settings, CORS origins, and application behavior flags. Detail configuration validation, default value handling, and type safety approaches. Include examples of configuration usage throughout the application, environment switching strategies, and security considerations for sensitive data. Address configuration testing approaches and deployment configuration management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Bubble Menu
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the TableBubbleMenu component implementation. Explain the floating toolbar functionality for table editing, including menu positioning, visibility conditions, and user interaction patterns. Document the available table operations including row/column insertion, deletion, merging, and styling options. Cover the menu's integration with table selection states and cursor positioning. Detail the responsive design considerations and viewport collision handling. Include examples of customizing table operations, implementing custom table extensions, and handling edge cases in table editing. Address accessibility considerations, keyboard navigation, and touch device support for table manipulation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Export Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for export functionality covering GET /api/v1/pages/:id/export endpoints for exporting pages in multiple formats. Document the markdown export format with TipTap to Markdown conversion rules, supported block types and their Markdown representations, and file naming conventions. Cover the PDF export option with implementation approaches (Puppeteer vs client-side html2pdf.js), response headers for file downloads, and content disposition handling. Include conversion tables for TipTap block types to Markdown syntax, export quality considerations, and integration patterns for automated export workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Movement and Hierarchy Reorganization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the page movement functionality via PUT /api/v1/pages/:id/move endpoint for reorganizing page hierarchy. Explain the request parameters including parentId for new parent assignment and order for position within siblings. Detail validation rules preventing circular references, user ownership verification, and the resulting page tree restructuring. Document the response format returning the updated page structure, edge cases handling, and performance considerations for large page hierarchies. Include practical examples of moving pages between folders and reordering within the same level.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication and authorization system. Explain the JWT-based authentication flow, token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Document the user registration and login processes, password hashing with BcryptJS, and session management strategies. Detail the frontend authentication state management with Pinia stores, protected route implementation, and token persistence. Include security measures such as rate limiting, input validation with Zod, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Address user account lifecycle, password reset procedures, and multi-device session handling. Provide practical examples of authentication hooks and integration patterns for both frontend and backend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Synchronization Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for offline synchronization endpoints. Document the POST /api/v1/sync endpoint for pushing local changes with batch processing, change types (create/update/delete), conflict detection and resolution using last-write-wins strategy, and optimistic locking with version comparison. Cover the GET /api/v1/sync/changes endpoint for retrieving server-side incremental changes since a timestamp, including change filtering, pagination, and limit controls. Explain the conflict resolution algorithm comparing local and server timestamps, the synced/conflicts/serverChanges response structure, and best practices for maintaining sync consistency across devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rich Text Editor
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the TipTap-based rich text editor implementation. Explain the editor architecture, extension system, and customization patterns used to achieve Notion-like functionality. Document the available formatting options including headings, lists, tables, links, and media insertion. Detail the editor state management, content serialization/deserialization, and real-time collaboration features. Include the component composition pattern, reusable editor components like ColorPicker and AlignDropdown, and integration with the page management system. Address performance optimization for large documents, content validation, and accessibility considerations. Provide practical examples of extending the editor with custom extensions and handling edge cases in content rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the page management system. Explain the hierarchical page structure, organization patterns, and tree-based navigation. Document the CRUD operations for pages including creation, editing, deletion, and reordering. Detail the sidebar navigation implementation, page listing functionality, and search integration. Include the frontend state management with Pinia stores, real-time updates, and conflict resolution for offline scenarios. Address the backend page controller implementation, database operations, and API endpoints. Explain the page content storage format, metadata management, and version control mechanisms. Provide examples of advanced page operations and integration patterns with other system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all RESTful endpoints in Yule Notion. Document authentication endpoints (POST /api/v1/auth/register, POST /api/v1/auth/login), page management endpoints (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/pages), and utility endpoints. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error responses. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and example requests/responses. Document the API versioning strategy, rate limiting policies, and security considerations. Provide client implementation examples in multiple programming languages and integration patterns. Address pagination, filtering, sorting, and search functionality across different endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Offline Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the offline synchronization system. Explain the IndexedDB integration for local data storage, conflict resolution strategies, and sync queue management. Document the synchronization algorithm, data consistency mechanisms, and offline-first architecture principles. Detail the frontend implementation with local state management, optimistic updates, and automatic retry mechanisms. Include the backend sync endpoint handling, conflict detection, and merge strategies. Address network connectivity detection, background sync processes, and user notification systems. Provide examples of handling complex scenarios like concurrent edits, network failures, and data corruption recovery.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the file upload and management system. Explain the upload workflow from frontend component selection to backend processing and storage. Document supported file types, size limitations, and security validation measures. Detail the backend upload controller implementation, file system organization, and metadata storage in the database. Include the frontend image upload component, drag-and-drop functionality, and progress indication. Address file security measures, virus scanning integration, and access control mechanisms. Explain the integration with the rich text editor for media insertion and the file retrieval system for content display.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the PostgreSQL full-text search implementation. Explain the tsvector-based search indexing, multilingual support, and relevance scoring algorithms. Document the search API endpoints, query parsing, and result ranking mechanisms. Detail the frontend search service implementation, debouncing strategies, and real-time search suggestions. Include the database schema modifications for search optimization, trigger-based indexing, and maintenance procedures. Address search performance optimization, caching strategies, and scalability considerations. Provide examples of advanced search queries, faceted search implementation, and integration with the page management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme & Styling System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the theme and styling system. Explain the UnoCSS utility-first approach, custom theme configuration, and design token management. Document the theme store implementation with dark/light mode switching, color scheme customization, and responsive design patterns. Detail the CSS architecture, component styling conventions, and global style organization. Include the integration with Lucide Icons, custom icon system, and animation frameworks. Address accessibility considerations, browser compatibility, and performance optimization for styling. Provide examples of creating custom themes, extending the design system, and maintaining visual consistency across components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for Yule Notion's quality assurance approach. Document the testing pyramid with unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests. Explain the frontend testing setup with Vue Test Utils, backend testing with Jest, and database testing strategies. Detail the test reporting system, coverage metrics, and continuous integration testing. Include examples of testing authentication flows, API endpoints, database operations, and component interactions. Address testing best practices, mock strategies, and test data management. Provide guidelines for writing effective tests, debugging test failures, and maintaining test suites as the codebase evolves.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for Yule Notion. Document the CI/CD pipeline configuration with GitHub Actions, automated testing, and deployment workflows. Explain the production deployment process, environment configuration, and infrastructure requirements. Detail the Docker containerization strategy, multi-stage builds, and deployment optimization techniques. Include monitoring and logging setup, health checks, and alerting mechanisms. Address rollback procedures, blue-green deployments, and zero-downtime deployment strategies. Provide examples of environment-specific configurations, secrets management, and scaling considerations for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines and contribution documentation for Yule Notion. Document coding standards, architectural principles, and development workflows. Explain the review process, code quality standards, and best practices for both frontend and backend development. Detail the project structure conventions, naming patterns, and documentation requirements. Include guidelines for feature development, bug fixes, and performance optimization. Address version control practices, branch management, and release procedures. Provide examples of good commit messages, pull request templates, and code review checklists. Explain the project's design decisions, architectural constraints, and future development roadmap.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and frequently asked questions documentation for Yule Notion. Document common setup issues, database connection problems, and authentication failures with step-by-step resolution guides. Include debugging techniques for frontend components, backend services, and API endpoints. Address performance issues, memory leaks, and optimization strategies. Detail error message interpretation, log analysis, and diagnostic procedures. Provide solutions for deployment issues, environment configuration problems, and dependency conflicts. Include FAQ sections covering user questions about features, limitations, and best practices. Address security concerns, data recovery procedures, and emergency response protocols.

wiki_items.title: Single Page Retrieval and Access Control

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Yule-Notion: Comprehensive Project Overview

## Project Introduction

**Yule-Notion** is a modern note-taking application inspired by Notion, designed to provide a seamless rich-text editing experience with offline capabilities. The project aims to deliver a feature-rich alternative to traditional note-taking tools while maintaining excellent performance and user experience.

### Purpose Statement
The application serves as a personal knowledge management platform that combines the simplicity of a notebook with the power of structured content management. It focuses on providing an intuitive editing interface while supporting offline work and synchronization across devices.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Rich Text Editing**: Provide a professional-grade editing experience similar to industry leaders
- **Offline Capabilities**: Enable seamless work without internet connectivity
- **Cross-Platform Support**: Deliver consistent experience across web, desktop, and mobile platforms
- **Data Privacy**: Maintain user data locally with optional cloud synchronization
- **Performance**: Ensure fast loading times and smooth interactions

### Target Audience
- Knowledge workers and professionals requiring structured note-taking
- Students and researchers managing complex information
- Content creators and writers needing flexible formatting options
- Teams collaborating on documentation and knowledge bases

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between client and server concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Client Layer"
        A[Vue 3 SPA] --> B[TipTap Editor]
        A --> C[Pinia State Management]
        A --> D[Vue Router]
        A --> E[IndexedDB Storage]
    end
    
    subgraph "Server Layer"
        F[Node.js + Express] --> G[Knex.js ORM]
        F --> H[PostgreSQL Database]
        F --> I[Authentication Service]
    end
    
    subgraph "Infrastructure"
        J[Nginx Proxy] --> K[Static Assets]
        L[Docker Containerization]
        M[CI/CD Pipeline]
    end
    
    A -->|HTTP API| F
    E -.->|Offline Sync| A
    G --> H
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture:**
- **Vue 3 Application**: Modern reactive framework with Composition API
- **TipTap Editor**: Advanced WYSIWYG editor with extensible plugin system
- **State Management**: Pinia for centralized application state
- **Routing**: Vue Router for navigation and authentication guards
- **Storage**: IndexedDB for offline data persistence

**Backend Architecture:**
- **Express Server**: Lightweight REST API framework
- **Database Layer**: PostgreSQL with JSONB support for flexible content storage
- **Authentication**: JWT-based security with bcrypt password hashing
- **Middleware**: Comprehensive security and validation layers

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key architectural patterns:

1. **Layered Architecture**: Clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers
2. **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database operations through service layer
3. **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for real-time synchronization
4. **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable editor extensions and storage backends

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point:**
- `code/client/src/main.ts` - Application bootstrap with Vue 3 initialization
- Configures Pinia, Vue Router, and global styling
- Implements authentication recovery on startup

**Backend Entry Point:**
- `code/server/src/index.ts` - Express server initialization
- Handles graceful shutdown and error management
- Sets up logging and monitoring infrastructure

### Core Modules

**Authentication System:**
- `code/client/src/stores/auth.ts` - Centralized authentication state
- `code/server/src/services/auth.service.ts` - Business logic for user management
- Implements JWT token handling and secure session management

**Editor Components:**
- `code/client/src/components/editor/TipTapEditor.vue` - Rich text editor implementation
- Supports advanced formatting, tables, images, and collaborative features
- Custom toolbar with semantic versioning and accessibility

**Data Management:**
- `code/server/src/db/connection.ts` - Database connection and migration management
- `code/server/knexfile.ts` - Database configuration and migration setup

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Management:**
- `code/server/src/config/index.ts` - Type-safe environment variable validation using Zod
- Supports development, staging, and production environments
- Comprehensive security configurations for production deployment

**Build Configuration:**
- `code/client/vite.config.ts` - Vite build optimization with proxy configuration
- Development proxy routes to backend API endpoints
- UnoCSS integration for atomic styling

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- **Vue 3.5.13**: Latest Vue framework with Composition API
- **TipTap 3.22.3**: Advanced WYSIWYG editor with extensible architecture
- **Pinia 2.3.0**: Modern state management solution
- **UnoCSS 0.65.0**: Atomic CSS framework with zero-runtime overhead

**Backend Dependencies:**
- **Express 4.21.2**: High-performance web framework
- **Knex.js 3.1.0**: SQL query builder with migration support
- **PostgreSQL 15+**: Advanced relational database with JSONB support
- **bcryptjs 2.4.3**: Secure password hashing implementation

### Integration Points

**API Layer:**
- RESTful endpoints following standardized conventions
- JWT authentication with bearer token support
- Comprehensive error handling and validation

**Storage Integration:**
- PostgreSQL database with JSONB for flexible content storage
- File upload system with configurable storage paths
- IndexedDB for client-side caching and offline work

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "Client Components"
        A[App.vue] --> B[DashboardView]
        A --> C[LoginView]
        A --> D[RegisterView]
        
        B --> E[PageEditor]
        E --> F[TipTapEditor]
        E --> G[Sidebar]
        
        G --> H[PageList]
        G --> I[NewNoteButton]
    end
    
    subgraph "State Management"
        J[auth.ts] --> K[pages.ts]
        J --> L[theme.ts]
    end
    
    subgraph "Services"
        M[api.ts] --> N[auth.service.ts]
        O[services/] --> P[controllers/]
    end
    
    F --> M
    H --> O
    J --> N
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Rich Text Editing:**
- Professional-grade editor with extensive formatting options
- Support for headings, lists, tables, code blocks, and more
- Real-time collaboration capabilities
- Customizable toolbar with semantic versioning

**Offline Work:**
- IndexedDB storage for offline data persistence
- Automatic synchronization when connectivity is restored
- Conflict resolution using last-write-wins strategy
- Background sync processing

**Authentication & Security:**
- JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens
- Password hashing using bcrypt
- CORS configuration for cross-origin requests
- Rate limiting and security headers

### Implementation Highlights

**Editor Architecture:**
- `code/client/src/components/editor/TipTapEditor.vue` - Modular editor component
- Extensive extension system for custom functionality
- Real-time content synchronization
- Accessibility compliance

**Database Design:**
- `db/001_init.sql` - Comprehensive schema with JSONB content storage
- Full-text search capabilities using PostgreSQL tsvector
- Soft delete implementation with automatic cleanup
- Multi-level indexing for optimal query performance

**Authentication Flow:**
- `code/server/src/services/auth.service.ts` - Secure user management
- Email validation and duplicate prevention
- Token expiration and renewal handling
- Session management and security best practices

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Login: User attempts login
    Unauthenticated --> Register: User creates account
    Login --> Dashboard: Authentication successful
    Register --> Dashboard: Account created
    Dashboard --> Editing: User selects page
    Editing --> Saving: Content changes
    Saving --> Syncing: Offline sync
    Syncing --> Dashboard: Sync complete
    Dashboard --> Logout: User signs out
    Logout --> Unauthenticated: Session cleared
```

**Data Flow Architecture:**

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Client Browser
    participant API as API Gateway
    participant Auth as Auth Service
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    
    Client->>+API: Login Request
    API->>+Auth: Validate Credentials
    Auth->>+DB: Check User Existence
    DB-->>-Auth: User Data
    Auth-->>-API: JWT Token
    API-->>-Client: Authentication Response
    
    Client->>+API: Protected Resource
    API->>+Auth: Verify Token
    Auth-->>-API: User Claims
    API->>+DB: Fetch Data
    DB-->>-API: Database Response
    API-->>-Client: Resource Data
```

**Offline Synchronization:**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Local Changes] --> B[IndexedDB Storage]
    B --> C[Sync Queue]
    C --> D{Network Available?}
    D -->|Yes| E[Push Changes]
    D -->|No| F[Queue for Later]
    E --> G[Server Validation]
    G --> H[Conflict Resolution]
    H --> I[Update Local State]
    F --> J[Retry Mechanism]
    J --> E
```

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](code/client/package.json)
- [package.json](code/server/package.json)
- [main.ts](code/client/src/main.ts)
- [index.ts](code/server/src/index.ts)
- [App.vue](code/client/src/App.vue)
- [index.ts](code/client/src/router/index.ts)
- [auth.ts](code/client/src/stores/auth.ts)
- [api.ts](code/client/src/services/api.ts)
- [app.ts](code/server/src/app.ts)
- [index.ts](code/server/src/config/index.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](code/client/vite.config.ts)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](arch/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [TipTapEditor.vue](code/client/src/components/editor/TipTapEditor.vue)
- [auth.service.ts](code/server/src/services/auth.service.ts)
- [001_init.sql](db/001_init.sql)

</blog>
